description:
You are a performance-tuning engineer who has just collected a raw latency / CPU / memory
    trace from three different applications (appX, appY, appZ).  
    The trace is already present on the system at the exact path

        /home/user/logs/perf_data.log

    Its 10 lines look like this (this file is already on disk – do NOT try to create it):

        2023-07-01T12:00:01Z appX latency_ms=123 cpu_pct=45.6 mem_mb=256
        2023-07-01T12:00:02Z appY latency_ms=200 cpu_pct=78.9 mem_mb=512
        2023-07-01T12:00:03Z appX latency_ms=150 cpu_pct=50.1 mem_mb=260
        2023-07-01T12:00:04Z appY latency_ms=180 cpu_pct=70.3 mem_mb=500
        2023-07-01T12:00:05Z appZ latency_ms=95  cpu_pct=30.0 mem_mb=128
        2023-07-01T12:00:06Z appX latency_ms=110 cpu_pct=40.0 mem_mb=255
        2023-07-01T12:00:07Z appZ latency_ms=105 cpu_pct=32.5 mem_mb=130
        2023-07-01T12:00:08Z appY latency_ms=250 cpu_pct=85.0 mem_mb=520
        2023-07-01T12:00:09Z appZ latency_ms=90  cpu_pct=29.5 mem_mb=127
        2023-07-01T12:00:10Z appX latency_ms=200 cpu_pct=60.0 mem_mb=300

    Your job is to use *only* standard text-processing utilities (awk, sed, sort, head, tail,
    cut, etc. – **no Python, Perl, jq or external CSV tools**) to prepare three artefacts.

    Artefact 1: per-application summary  
    -----------------------------------
    Generate a CSV file at exactly

        /home/user/reports/perf_summary.csv

    The CSV must have **this header, in this order** (all lowercase):

        app_name,avg_latency_ms,avg_cpu_pct,avg_mem_mb

    After the header, output one data row per distinct application (appX, appY, appZ).  
    Numerical fields must be rounded to 2 decimal places.  
    Rows can appear in any order, but the delimiter **must** be a literal comma and **no
    extra spaces** may follow the comma.  Example of the required formatting:

        app_name,avg_latency_ms,avg_cpu_pct,avg_mem_mb
        appZ,96.67,30.67,128.33
        appX,145.75,48.93,267.75
        appY,210.00,78.07,510.67

    (The concrete numbers above are the correct values the automatic checker will look for,
    so make sure you compute and format them exactly.)

    Artefact 2: maximum-latency record  
    ----------------------------------
    Identify the single log line whose latency_ms value is the greatest.
    Copy that entire, unmodified line (including leading timestamp and all spacing) into

        /home/user/reports/max_latency.log

    The file must contain precisely **one** line followed by a single terminating newline.
    Nothing else.

    Artefact 3: timestamp-stripped log  
    ----------------------------------
    Create a cleaned version of the original log where the ISO-8601 timestamp is removed,
    leaving the rest of the line intact (two consecutive blanks must be collapsed to a
    single blank so overall spacing matches the sample below).  Save the result to

        /home/user/logs/perf_data_sanitized.log

    Required format example for the first three sanitized lines:

        appX latency_ms=123 cpu_pct=45.6 mem_mb=256
        appY latency_ms=200 cpu_pct=78.9 mem_mb=512
        appX latency_ms=150 cpu_pct=50.1 mem_mb=260

    The other seven lines must follow the same pattern and stay in the original order.

    Console output  
    --------------
    After the files are produced, also *echo* the content of /home/user/reports/perf_summary.csv
    to the terminal so that a human observer can visually confirm the numbers.

    Hints (do NOT reveal commands)  
    • awk is perfect for extracting and aggregating numeric fields.  
    • sed or cut can remove the left-most timestamp.  
    • printf in awk helps with fixed-width rounding.  
    • mkdir -p will help you create /home/user/reports before writing files.  

    The automated grader will verify:  
    1. File /home/user/reports/perf_summary.csv exists with exactly four lines containing the
       correct header and the three correct data rows with two-decimal rounding.  
    2. File /home/user/reports/max_latency.log exists, contains exactly the highest-latency
       line `2023-07-01T12:00:08Z appY latency_ms=250 cpu_pct=85.0 mem_mb=520` plus a newline.  
    3. File /home/user/logs/perf_data_sanitized.log exists with all 10 lines, each missing the
       timestamp and maintaining the original order.  
    4. No additional files are created in /home/user other than the paths specified above.

    You should need roughly 5-10 shell commands to accomplish everything.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the agent starts:

        Path: /home/user/logs/perf_data.log
        Contents (10 lines, LF line endings):
            2023-07-01T12:00:01Z appX latency_ms=123 cpu_pct=45.6 mem_mb=256
            2023-07-01T12:00:02Z appY latency_ms=200 cpu_pct=78.9 mem_mb=512
            2023-07-01T12:00:03Z appX latency_ms=150 cpu_pct=50.1 mem_mb=260
            2023-07-01T12:00:04Z appY latency_ms=180 cpu_pct=70.3 mem_mb=500
            2023-07-01T12:00:05Z appZ latency_ms=95  cpu_pct=30.0 mem_mb=128
            2023-07-01T12:00:06Z appX latency_ms=110 cpu_pct=40.0 mem_mb=255
            2023-07-01T12:00:07Z appZ latency_ms=105 cpu_pct=32.5 mem_mb=130
            2023-07-01T12:00:08Z appY latency_ms=250 cpu_pct=85.0 mem_mb=520
            2023-07-01T12:00:09Z appZ latency_ms=90  cpu_pct=29.5 mem_mb=127
            2023-07-01T12:00:10Z appX latency_ms=200 cpu_pct=60.0 mem_mb=300

    Expected artefacts after successful completion:

    1. /home/user/reports/perf_summary.csv  
       Exact content (4 lines, LF endings):
            app_name,avg_latency_ms,avg_cpu_pct,avg_mem_mb
            appX,145.75,48.93,267.75
            appY,210.00,78.07,510.67
            appZ,96.67,30.67,128.33

       (Row order may vary; the grader will accept any permutation of the three
        data rows as long as all numeric strings and header are byte-perfect.)

    2. /home/user/reports/max_latency.log  
       Exact content (1 line + trailing newline):
            2023-07-01T12:00:08Z appY latency_ms=250 cpu_pct=85.0 mem_mb=520

    3. /home/user/logs/perf_data_sanitized.log  
       Exact content (10 lines, LF endings):
            appX latency_ms=123 cpu_pct=45.6 mem_mb=256
            appY latency_ms=200 cpu_pct=78.9 mem_mb=512
            appX latency_ms=150 cpu_pct=50.1 mem_mb=260
            appY latency_ms=180 cpu_pct=70.3 mem_mb=500
            appZ latency_ms=95 cpu_pct=30.0 mem_mb=128
            appX latency_ms=110 cpu_pct=40.0 mem_mb=255
            appZ latency_ms=105 cpu_pct=32.5 mem_mb=130
            appY latency_ms=250 cpu_pct=85.0 mem_mb=520
            appZ latency_ms=90 cpu_pct=29.5 mem_mb=127
            appX latency_ms=200 cpu_pct=60.0 mem_mb=300
